Name: information_theoretic_neural_compression
Title: Operational Bounds for Neural Network Compression: An Information-Theoretic Approach
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the fundamental limits of neural network compression can be characterized using information-theoretic tools, specifically by analyzing the trade-off between compression rate and distortion in the representation of neural network weights and activations.
Related Work: Recent works such as Spectral Quantization Routing and Geometric Signal Separation have explored the use of geometric and information-theoretic methods for neural network compression and robustness. Our proposal builds upon these ideas by providing a more comprehensive information-theoretic framework for analyzing and optimizing neural network compression.
Abstract: This proposal seeks to establish operational bounds for neural network compression using information-theoretic tools. We aim to characterize the fundamental limits of compression in neural networks by analyzing the trade-off between compression rate and distortion in the representation of neural network weights and activations. Our approach will leverage techniques from rate-distortion theory and statistical learning theory to derive bounds on the achievable compression rates and distortion levels. We will also explore the use of mutual information and other information-theoretic measures to guide the compression process and optimize the trade-off between compression rate and accuracy.
Experiments: Derive operational bounds for neural network compression using rate-distortion theory
Evaluate the performance of existing compression methods using information-theoretic metrics
Develop and test new compression algorithms that optimize the trade-off between compression rate and distortion using mutual information and other information-theoretic measures
Investigate the application of our framework to various neural network architectures and tasks
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of neural network models and the difficulty of analyzing their compression properties
The challenge of developing effective compression algorithms that balance compression rate and distortion
The potential limitations of information-theoretic approaches in capturing the nuances of neural network behavior
